Quarterly Business Review

Presentation title with a
blue emphasis phrase

Subtitle or tagline — one sentence that frames the deck.

Agenda

What we will cover

1
Where we stand
Key metrics and highlights of the quarter
2
What we learned
Analysis, experiments, and customer signals
3
Where we go next

Presentation title 2 / 14
01
Section

Where we stand

Presentation title 3 / 14
Results

The quarter in three numbers

Payback period
5 Months
Implementation fully paid for itself; satisfaction, quality and compliance increased.
Net present value
120.000 €
2,700 documents over five years generated a net benefit of 120.000 € after costs.
ROI
240%
Return on invest calculated for a three-year period after implementation.
Context: figures are audited actuals from the pilot deployment, not projections.
Presentation title 4 / 14
Analysis

Bullets carry facts, the card carries the point

  • First key point about the topic, stated as a finding rather than a topic label.
  • Second key point with supporting detail — one line of context is enough here.
  • Third point that sets up the insight card on the right side.
Key insight
Automation quality is a compounding asset — every corrected document trains the next thousand.
Presentation title 5 / 14
Benefits

How the business profits

Speed up procedures
Machines work at full speed around the clock, expediting every process they touch.
Enhanced efficiency
Tasks complete more swiftly, lowering operational expenses across departments.
Unleashed productivity
Freeing the workplace from mind-numbing repetitive processes creates room for value.
Presentation title 6 / 14
Process

From analysis to running machine

Step 01
Analysis
Capture existing business processes and identify those with high automation potential.
Step 02
Design
Create a workflow that represents process sequences and covers decision points.
Step 03
Implementation
Implement and test the machines on the chosen digital environment.
Step 04
Maintenance
Continuously monitor the machines to ensure smooth operation.
Presentation title 7 / 14
Comparison

Performance by production line

LineOwnerEfficiencyQualityUptimeΔ vs Q1
Line AOperations87.3%99.2%94.1%+2.1 pp
Line BOperations82.1%99.5%91.8%+0.8 pp
Line CLogistics89.7%98.8%96.3%+4.6 pp
Line DLogistics78.4%99.1%89.5%−1.2 pp
Observation: Line C leads on efficiency after last quarter's process improvements.

Data

Quarterly performance, visualized

Architecture

How a document flows through

Plan

Delivery roadmap

JulAugSepOctNovDec
Discovery
Process capture
Design
Workflows + schema
Implementation
Build + integrate
Pilot
Line C
Go-live
Dependency: ERP sandbox access must be granted before implementation starts in October.
Presentation title 11 / 14
“
The machines took over our repetitive work in weeks, not quarters — and the team finally has time for the problems worth solving.
Christopher Menzel · CEO, Universidade Federal de Pelotas
Presentation title 12 / 14
Deep dive

Cost per document, falling fast

  • Cost falls as correction feedback accumulates.
  • Human review needed for 2% of documents by week 20.
  • Break-even against manual processing at week 9.
Presentation title 13 / 14

Thank you.
